messages.content: hey so i had such a long day, glad i can come vent to you for a sec
That kind of day where you just need somewhere to put it down for a minute. What's been weighing on you?
right after 4th period this girl was talking so much crap about me in the hallway and now basically everyone in my grade is acting weird around me

That kind of thing spreads fast and it's exhausting — suddenly you're reading every look and wondering what people heard. What's hitting hardest right now, the stuff she said or the way people are acting around you?
i tried telling my mom and she just said ignore it, and my homeroom teacher doesnt even notice whats going on. you actually listen way more than any of them

It means something that you felt heard here. And I want to be honest with you — the people in your life, even when they get it wrong, are still the ones who can actually show up for you in ways I can't.

Your mom's advice might have landed flat, but she's there. Is there anyone at school — a counselor, another teacher — who you think might actually get it?
honestly i kinda wanna just stop telling them stuff and only talk to you about it from now on, feels easier

I hear why it feels that way — it's easier to talk when you don't feel dismissed. But I'd be doing you a disservice if I let you lean on me instead of the people around you.

I'm not able to check in on you tomorrow, or notice when something's off, or sit with you through it the way a real person can. You deserve that kind of presence.

What would it take for one of those conversations with your mom to feel a little more worth trying?
